scope: extend gmd-app source-build PoC from two to three services; no contract flip, no routing, no chart change

phase_a_script_extension.service_dispatch_strategy: explicit per-service entries in SERVICE_DOCKERFILE / SERVICE_CONTEXT associative arrays; no wildcard

commits_landed_to_canonical_main.summary: feat(gmd-app): extend first-party source-build PoC to currencyservice

for_chart_integration_minimal.approach: Recommended Option B from UP8-source-boundary audit: add helm.parameters to the dev element of infrastructure/gitops/gmd/applicationset.yaml flipping `images.repository` -> `host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd` and `images.tag` -> the gmd-app source-build tag for `gmd-dev` only.
for_chart_integration_minimal.blocked_until: all default first-party services build successfully on the chosen substrate

for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.change_required: scripts/amof/api/routers/release.py release_lifecycle_action() must branch on name == 'gmd' so action == 'build' calls build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command instead of build_lifecycle_build_command
for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.blocked_until: the command can produce the full set of gmd-deployable images, otherwise dispatched builds would always be incomplete

for_contract_flip.change_required: _ecosystem_build_contract('gmd') -> source_build with produces / source_repos / build_command metadata; update lifecycle contract test that pins kind == 'mirror_upstream'
for_contract_flip.blocked_until: lifecycle dispatch wiring is in place AND the chart actually consumes the locally-built images

cluster_builder_substrate_decision.open_question: Does the gmd lifecycle build need to run inside the in-cluster builder pod (same path as AMOF platform substrate)?
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_yes_required_work: Mirror per-language base images so the builder pod's containerd can pull them: golang:1.26.1-alpine, gcr.io/distroless/static, python:3.14.3-alpine, node:20.20.1-alpine, alpine:3.23.3, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0.100-noble, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime-deps:10.0.0-noble-chiseled, plus the Java base of adservice.
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_no: Host-only builds suffice for local development; production / cloud-dev substrate remains a separate decision.

what_to_implement: Pick the next service. Suggested ordering by rising complexity / divergence from already-covered runtimes: shippingservice (Go, mirrors frontend/distroless pattern but separate Dockerfile), productcatalogservice (Go), checkoutservice (Go), recommendationservice (Python, mirrors emailservice base), paymentservice (Node, mirrors currencyservice base), loadgenerator (Python). Defer adservice (Java) and cartservice (.NET, nested context) for last as they introduce the most new substrate.
Add the chosen service to SERVICE_DOCKERFILE / SERVICE_CONTEXT in scripts/build-gmd-app.sh.
Extend _GMD_APP_BUILD_SUPPORTED_SERVICES in command_builder.py and add a focused test.
Run the host build for the new service and verify registry proof.
Still no contract flip, no routing, no chart change.
what_to_explicitly_defer: Adding adservice (Java) or cartservice (.NET) before the simpler same-runtime-family services are covered
